isolation.authState: not signed in (read-only via public sheet URL)

assertions.title: Missing discovery endpoint opens setup instead of posting

steps.action: Load app with ?sheet= parameter and no saved discovery endpoint
steps.expected: Dashboard visible with Run discovery button
steps.observed: Dashboard visible after manually bypassing sheet-access gate; Run discovery button present but DISABLED

steps.expected: Discovery setup flow opens; no POST sent
steps.observed: Button is disabled (HTML disabled attribute). Click does nothing. No setup wizard opens and no POST is sent.

assertions.issues: The 'Run discovery' button has the HTML disabled attribute when no discovery endpoint is configured, so clicking it fires no event. The code's click handler (initDiscoveryButton) would open the setup wizard if the button were enabled, but syncDiscoveryButtonState disables it in the else branch. The tooltip says 'click to open discovery setup' but the disabled attribute prevents clicks.

assertions.title: Test webhook uses unsaved form values and the v1 payload

steps.action: Open Settings > Discovery tab, type https://example.com/webhook WITHOUT saving
steps.observed: Field populated with typed URL; no save performed

steps.expected: POST sent to the typed (unsaved) URL with v1 payload fields
steps.observed: Browser attempted fetch to https://example.com/webhook (blocked by CORS preflight, confirming the request was sent to the unsaved URL). Payload builder confirmed to include event, schemaVersion, sheetId, variationKey, requestedAt, discoveryProfile.

evidence.consoleErrors: CORS error on https://example.com/webhook (expected — confirms POST was attempted)
evidence.network: OPTIONS preflight to https://example.com/webhook blocked by CORS — confirms browser sent request to unsaved URL

assertions.title: Browser validation rejects invalid browser endpoints client-side

steps.action: Enter http://localhost:9999/webhook in discovery endpoint field and click Test webhook
steps.expected: Rejected client-side with remediation text, no request sent
steps.observed: Toast: 'Localhost URLs won't work here. Use your Worker URL or another public HTTPS endpoint.' No network request sent.

steps.action: Enter http://example.com/webhook (non-HTTPS) and click Test webhook
steps.expected: Rejected client-side with remediation text, no request sent
steps.observed: Toast: 'URL must use HTTPS. HTTP endpoints won't work — use HTTPS.' No network request sent.

steps.action: Enter https://some-worker.workers.dev/forward and click Test webhook
steps.expected: Rejected client-side with remediation text, no request sent
steps.observed: Toast: 'Use the root Worker URL, not /forward. The /forward path requires auth headers the browser doesn't send.' No network request sent.

evidence.network: no requests sent for any of the three invalid URLs

assertions.title: Managed Apps Script stays marked as stub-only

steps.action: Enter https://script.google.com/macros/s/fake-test-id/exec in discovery endpoint field and click Test webhook
steps.expected: Verification succeeds as stub_only with warning copy
steps.observed: CORS error on preflight. Toast: 'Apps Script is deployed, but this browser hit CORS. Cloudflare relay agent setup is open.' The fake URL does not have a real deployed Apps Script behind it, so stub_only classification cannot be tested.

evidence.network: OPTIONS preflight to script.google.com blocked by CORS

assertions.issues: Cannot fully test stub_only classification without a real deployed managed Apps Script stub. The code has the stub_only classification path (DISCOVERY_ENGINE_STATE_STUB_ONLY, handleAppsScriptBrowserCorsFailure) but the fake URL triggers CORS before reaching that logic.

assertions.title: Browser-facing discovery endpoint must be the open Worker root URL

steps.action: Enter https://my-worker.workers.dev/ (open Worker root) and click Test webhook
steps.observed: No client-side rejection. Browser attempted DNS resolution (ERR_NAME_NOT_RESOLVED for fake domain). Toast: 'Can't reach the endpoint. Network or CORS error — check the URL and try again.' URL is allowed.

steps.action: Enter https://some-worker.workers.dev/forward (Worker /forward path) and click Test webhook
steps.observed: Rejected client-side: 'Use the root Worker URL, not /forward. The /forward path requires auth headers the browser doesn't send.'

steps.action: Enter https://example.com/api/discovery (generic HTTPS) and click Test webhook
steps.observed: No client-side rejection. Browser attempted fetch (CORS blocked). URL is allowed.

evidence.consoleErrors: ERR_NAME_NOT_RESOLVED for my-worker.workers.dev (fake domain); CORS error for example.com
evidence.network: requests attempted for Worker root and generic HTTPS; /forward blocked client-side before request

assertions.title: Test webhook and Run discovery share async-aware success semantics

steps.expected: Endpoint saved, Run discovery button enabled
steps.observed: Endpoint saved via Save & reload; Run discovery button enabled after reload

steps.action: Open Settings > Discovery, click Test webhook with saved endpoint
steps.observed: Toast: 'Can't reach the endpoint. Network or CORS error — check the URL and try again.' with 'Fix tunnel' action

steps.action: Click Run discovery > Run now with same endpoint
steps.expected: Same failure classification as Test webhook
steps.observed: Toast: 'Can't reach the endpoint. Network or CORS error — check the URL and try again.' with 'Fix tunnel' action — identical classification

evidence.network: both paths attempted fetch to https://example.com/api/discovery (CORS blocked both times)

assertions.title: Discovery entry points converge on the expected discovery surface

steps.action: Open Settings > Discovery tab > click 'Open discovery setup'
steps.observed: Discovery setup wizard opened with steps: Status, Path, Config, Server, Tunnel, Relay, Test, Done

steps.action: Click 'Open discovery setup wizard' button in header
steps.observed: Discovery setup wizard opened with same step structure

steps.action: Click 'Run discovery' button (with saved endpoint configured)
steps.expected: Discovery preferences/run modal opens (correct surface for configured endpoint)
steps.observed: Discovery preferences modal opened with Manual/AI Suggest tabs, preference fields, and Run now button

frictions.description: Sheet-access gate reappears after save-reload cycles. The gate shows 'Welcome back' and 'Log in with Google' even though the sheet is publicly readable (JSONP fetch succeeds with 78 rows). The gate check in loadAllData() blocks dashboard reveal when getOAuthClientId() is truthy and accessToken is falsy, even though the data loaded successfully via JSONP. Had to manually set dashboard display to block and gate display to none to proceed with testing.
frictions.resolution: Used eval to set document.getElementById('dashboard').style.display='block' and document.getElementById('sheetAccessGateScreen').style.display='none' and window.initialSheetAccessResolved=true

frictions.description: Toast overlays blocked button clicks in Settings modal. When toasts accumulated on screen, the Test webhook button became unclickable because the toast overlay intercepted pointer events.
frictions.resolution: Used eval to remove all .toast elements before clicking buttons

frictions.description: Cloudflare relay setup modal opened automatically after certain test webhook attempts and blocked interaction with the settings modal beneath it.
frictions.resolution: Used eval to set cloudflareRelaySetupModal display to none

blockers.description: VAL-DISCOVERY-004: Cannot test managed Apps Script stub-only classification without a real deployed Apps Script web app. The fake URL (https://script.google.com/macros/s/fake-test-id/exec) triggers CORS preflight failure before the stub_only classification logic runs. The code has the stub_only path but it requires the Apps Script to respond with the expected stub response shape.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Tried with fake Apps Script URL — CORS blocks preflight. Checked code logic for stub_only path — exists but unreachable without real deploy.

summary: Tested 7 assertions: 5 passed, 1 failed (VAL-DISCOVERY-001: Run discovery button is disabled when no endpoint is configured, preventing the click-to-open-setup flow), 1 blocked (VAL-DISCOVERY-004: cannot test Apps Script stub-only without real deploy). Key finding: the Run discovery button is HTML-disabled when no endpoint exists, contradicting the assertion that clicking it should open setup.
